narrative.summary: Serious bread bakery; crusty loaves and excellent croissants; also has Riverside location.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're in the heart of Kensington Market, among the vintage shops and produce stalls, where Blackbird's storefront opens onto a small bakery with a glass case full of burnished loaves. Inside, it's tight—a few seats if you're lucky, but most people grab and go. The real show is the bread itself, stacked on shelves behind the counter, and the pastries arranged like they've just been pulled from the oven.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is serious bread—crusty sourdough boules with crackled crusts, the kind you tear into with your hands. The pastries are just as committed: kouign-amann with layers that shatter, buckwheat financiers that taste earthy and buttery at once, croissants that pull apart in honeycomb sheets. There's also a lemon tart that locals return for, sharp and clean. Pastries run $4–7, loaves around $8–12.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Hit it Saturday morning right after opening—you'll get first pick of the pastries and a seat if you want one, then wander Kensington with a coffee in hand. Or stop by midweek to grab a loaf before they sell out.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Delicious baked goods in an interesting neighborhood! I had a drip coffee and a kouign-amann. As soon as I finished my pastry I wanted another. Definitely stop by if you are in Toronto and take a stroll around Kensington Market. Seating is limited but was no problem for us when we arrived shortly after opening on a Saturday."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "This bakery is special.   You can tell it is loved by the locals for the wide selection of fresh breads but it’s worth a stop as a visitor for the pastries.   The buckwheat financiers are exceptional.  An excellent lemon tart, and viennoiserie as well."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Very fresh daily baked bread 🥖 with love and kindness.  The sourdough breads must have to go!  Next time I’ll come back to get croissants 🥐"
